body: The implement agent should be able to create stacked PRs (or at least know which branch to start with rather than just merging to main only)

comments.body:
see the context for #51 

@sepo-agent /answer can you check the code and plan for a way for implementing it?

Feasible, and I’d implement #30 first because it is the branch primitive that #51’s meta/sub-orchestrator model will need.

What exists now:

- `agent-implement.yml` always checks out the default branch, creates the work branch from the default branch, and opens the PR against the default branch:
  - checkout: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-implement.yml#L120-L125
  - branch creation: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-implement.yml#L166-L167
  - PR base: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-implement.yml#L222-L233
- `create-pr.ts` already supports `BASE_BRANCH`; the workflow just hardcodes it to the default branch: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/cli/create-pr.ts#L12-L50
- `dispatch-agent-implement.ts` does not accept or forward any base branch input yet: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/cli/dispatch-agent-implement.ts#L10-L36
- The orchestrator currently dispatches `implement` without branch context: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/cli/orchestrate-handoff.ts#L366-L373
- #51’s broader orchestrator-to-orchestrator idea is not supported yet because `AgentAction` is limited to `implement | review | fix-pr`, and agent-mode planner decisions are validated against the fixed transition policy: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/handoff.ts#L3-L27 and https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/handoff.ts#L296-L333

Implementation plan for #30:

1. Add a `base_branch` input to `agent-implement.yml` for both `workflow_dispatch` and `workflow_call`, defaulting to `""`.
2. Resolve an effective base branch early in the workflow:
   - if `inputs.base_branch` is present, use it;
   - otherwise use `github.event.repository.default_branch`.
3. Validate the branch/ref before using it in shell commands. I’d prefer a small typed CLI helper under `.agent/src/cli/` rather than inline shell, matching the repo convention of keeping workflow logic thin.
4. Use the resolved base branch for:
   - `actions/checkout@v4` `ref`;
   - `git checkout -b "$BRANCH" "$BASE_BRANCH"` or the checked-out `HEAD`;
   - `BASE_BRANCH` passed to `create-pr.ts`.
5. Extend `dispatch-agent-implement.ts` to read and forward `BASE_BRANCH`.
6. Extend `orchestrate-handoff.ts` so an orchestrator-dispatched `implement` can forward a base branch when the handoff context supplies one. I’d keep the first version deterministic: accept an explicit `BASE_BRANCH` env/input, but do not let free-form planner text silently become a branch name.
7. Add tests in the existing coverage areas:
   - workflow shape tests in `envelope.test.ts` proving `base_branch` is exposed and wired through checkout/branch/PR creation;
   - dispatch CLI tests proving `base_branch` is forwarded;
   - orchestrator CLI tests proving implement handoff can pass `base_branch`;
   - validation tests for empty/default branch and unsafe branch names.
8. Update `.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md` and supported workflow docs to describe stacked implementation PRs and the default-branch fallback.

For #51 after #30 lands, I’d do a second phase:

1. Extend the planner contract from only `next_action` to structured handoff metadata, e.g. `{ next_action: "implement", target_number, base_branch }`.
2. Add an orchestrator action type only after the base-branch plumbing is stable.
3. Add `orchestrator_lane` or `orchestrator_id` if we need multiple orchestrators on the same issue; today `agent-orchestrator.yml` hardcodes lane `planner`, so same-issue sub-orchestrators would share continuity.
4. Keep the current one-child-per-handoff model initially; batched sub-orchestrators should be a later design because dedupe, round budget, and status aggregation get more complicated.

Clarifying question before coding: should `base_branch` be accepted only as a branch name, or should it also accept PR numbers like `base_pr: 123` and resolve that PR’s head branch automatically? My recommendation for the first PR is branch-name only, then add PR-number resolution once the basic stacked PR path is proven.

Yes, I agree it is low cost to support both, as long as we keep the machine-facing contract explicit.

I would **not** overload `base_branch` with a string like `base_pr: 123` inside the workflow input. That makes validation/parsing messier and turns a typed workflow input into a mini-language. Instead I’d define two optional inputs:

```yaml
base_branch: "" # direct branch name, e.g. agent/implement-issue-12/codex-123
base_pr: ""     # PR number whose head branch should become the base
```

Rules:

- If neither is set: use `github.event.repository.default_branch`.
- If `base_branch` is set: validate it and use it directly.
- If `base_pr` is set: resolve the PR head branch and use that as the base.
- If both are set: fail early with a clear error, unless we intentionally define precedence. I’d prefer failing to avoid accidental wrong stacks.
- For `base_pr`, only accept a plain positive integer like `123`, not `#123` or `base_pr: 123` at the workflow-input layer.

The implementation is small because the repo already has most of the primitives:

- `agent-implement.yml` currently hardcodes default branch for checkout, implementation branch creation, and PR base: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-implement.yml#L120-L167 and https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.github/workflows/agent-implement.yml#L222-L233
- `create-pr.ts` already respects `BASE_BRANCH`: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/cli/create-pr.ts#L12-L50
- `github.ts` already has `fetchPrMeta()`, including `headRef`, `state`, and `isCrossRepository`, which is exactly what `base_pr` needs: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/github.ts#L139-L156

Concrete plan:

1. Add `base_branch` and `base_pr` inputs to `agent-implement.yml` for both `workflow_dispatch` and `workflow_call`.
2. Add a small CLI, e.g. `.agent/src/cli/resolve-implementation-base.ts`, that reads `BASE_BRANCH`, `BASE_PR`, `DEFAULT_BRANCH`, and `GITHUB_REPOSITORY`.
3. For `base_pr`, call `fetchPrMeta(basePr)` and resolve to `headRef`.
4. Reject cross-repo PR heads for the first version, because checkout/push semantics get ambiguous.
5. Probably require the base PR to be open unless there is a strong reason to stack onto closed PR branches.
6. Export `BASE_BRANCH=<resolved>` to `GITHUB_ENV`.
7. Use that resolved base for checkout/branch creation and PR creation.
8. Extend `dispatch-agent-implement.ts` and orchestrator dispatch plumbing to forward both inputs.
9. Add tests for default branch fallback, direct `base_branch`, `base_pr` resolution, both-inputs error, invalid PR number, closed/cross-repo rejection.

For human/planner-facing syntax, we can still document:

```yaml
base_pr: 123
```

But that should be the structured planner/context format, not a value stuffed into `base_branch`.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `orchestrate` -> `implement` (round 2/10).

manual orchestrate start on issue; dispatching implement

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777524380908 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUxNDc4ODkyMjQ6b3JjaGVzdHJhdGU6MzA6aW1wbGVtZW50OjMwOjI -->

**Sepo implementation finished**

- Branch: `agent/implement-issue-30/codex-25147905607`
- Pull request: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/56

Implemented stacked implementation PR support by adding validated `base_branch` and `base_pr` inputs, resolving PR heads through a typed CLI, wiring the resolved base into branch creation and PR creation, forwarding base inputs through implement/orchestrator dispatch paths, and updating docs. Verification: `npm test` passed, and after the final workflow refspec tweak `npm run build && node --test dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js dist/__tests__/implementation-base.test.js dist/__tests__/dispatch-agent-implement-cli.test.js dist/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.js` passed.

title: More flexible branching strategy
